nativeRange.habitat: Shallow coastal reef zones and lagoons; typically found on coral rubble, sandy substrate, and hard substrate in the photic zone

imageCandidates.notes: Primary product photo from AlgaeBarn listing
Product photo from Mosaic Macros 'Red Bush Gracilaria' listing
Product photo from Top Shelf Aquatics 'Pom Pom Gracilaria' listing

summary: Bushy, spherical red macroalgae forming compact pom-pom-shaped clumps; popular for both refugium nutrient export and display aquascaping. Tends to grow quickly and generally considered among the easier macroalgae to keep.
careNotes: Pom Pom (Gracilaria hayi) is a distinctive red macroalgae forming tight, rounded bushy clumps — the morphology is the direct origin of its trade name. It is widely kept for dual purposes: as an ornamental display specimen and as a nutrient-export workhorse. AlgaeBarn describes the fronds as sharply tapered and flame-like, with fast enough growth to require periodic harvesting to prevent system nutrient export from stalling. TSA notes it as a ravenous consumer of nitrates, phosphates, and dissolved organic wastes, and reports resistance to biofouling. Both placement in a refugium (gently tumbled with moderate flow) and direct placement in the display tank are commonly recommended. AlgaeBarn advises water temperature of 72–78°F (22–26°C), pH 8.1–8.4, salinity SG 1.023–1.025, and alkalinity 8–12 dKH; a secondary source (Fragbox) confirmed temperature 77–78°F and salinity 1.024–1.026, broadly consistent. Lighting is generally considered moderate — a standard 5000K reef fixture with an 8-hour photoperiod tends to suffice. Optional supplementation with magnesium, trace elements, and iron has been suggested by AlgaeBarn. The species is considered reef-safe, but most herbivorous fish (tangs, rabbitfish) will readily consume it; in tanks with dedicated herbivores it is generally used intentionally as a food source rather than as a display specimen. Mosaic Macros sells this species as 'Red Bush Gracilaria' in 2–3" plug cuttings and 5"+ cuttings. The species is also sold as 'Pom Pom Gracilaria' at Top Shelf Aquatics. Sibling Gracilaria species in the trade include Thick Ogo (Gracilaria sp.), Red Ogo (G. parvispora), Green Ogo (G. tikvahiae), Red Spaghetti (G. pacifica), and Monkey Finger (G. salicornia). Propagation is primarily by fragmentation; excess trimmings can be fed directly to herbivorous tankmates. Per Mosaic care guide guidance: as a red or brown alga, this species will MELT at the base if buried in sand — attach to rock via superglue, zip-tie/fishing line, or wedging instead. (Sand-bed acceptance per Mosaic is limited to Caulerpa, Halimeda, Penicillus, Rhipocephalus, and Udotea genera, plus some calcified Dasycladales.)

additional.name: Mosaic Macros
additional.notes: Confirmed species as Gracilaria hayi under trade name 'Red Bush Gracilaria'; minimal husbandry parameters. Sells as 2–3" plug and 5"+ cuttings.

additional.name: Top Shelf Aquatics
additional.notes: Confirmed nutrient export and display use; noted herbivore palatability. Minimal parameter data.

additional.name: Mosaic Macros general macroalgae care guide
additional.notes: Slice-wide secondary source — provides authoritative Mosaic-wide parameter targets (temp 22-26°C, pH 8.1-8.4, Ca 400-450 ppm, Mg 1250-1350 ppm, NO3 5-20 ppm, PO4 0.02-0.1 ppm) and the 4-method propagation standard. Added retroactively 2026-06-08.
